Phase 3B: Implement REST API, CLI args, and placeholder pipelines

- Created etl_core/api/ module with Flask REST API
  - endpoints: GET /api/pipelines, POST /api/execute, GET /api/jobs/<id>, etc.
  - Job queue with async execution and progress tracking
  - Rate limiting and comprehensive error handling

- Implemented Python API client (etl_core/api/client.py)
  - Simple interface for remote pipeline execution
  - Job status monitoring and result retrieval
  - Health check and job listing

- Created REST API server module (etl_core/api/server.py)
  - Standalone Flask app runner with configurable host/port
  - Integrated logging and error handling

- Added CLI argument parser (etl_core/cli/args.py)
  - Direct pipeline execution: --pipeline <id>
  - Config file loading: --config <file.json|file.yaml>
  - API server mode: --api --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000
  - Pipeline info and listing: --info <id>, --list

- Created placeholder pipeline modules (all COMING SOON)
  - amazon_manager: Multi-tenant Amazon DB manager
  - price_list: Rules-based price list (430+ profiles)
  - catalog_merger: 2-file catalog merger
  - tim_merger: 3-file TIM merger

- Added comprehensive test suites (56 new tests)
  - test_rest_api.py: 30 tests for API endpoints and client
  - test_cli_args.py: 26 tests for argument parsing and direct execution
  - All tests passing (100% pass rate)

- Updated requirements.txt with pyyaml for YAML config support

- Updated etl_core/cli/registry.py with helper functions
  - get_all_pipelines(): Returns all registered pipelines
  - get_pipeline_metadata(): Safe metadata lookup (returns None vs exception)

Test Results:
- 56 new integration tests for Phase 3B (all passing)
- 226 existing tests still passing (backward compatible)
- Total: 282 tests, 100% pass rate

Phase 3B enables:
- Remote pipeline execution via REST API
- Async job management with progress tracking
- Multiple ways to run pipelines (CLI menu, direct args, REST API)
- Foundation for web dashboard and monitoring

commit.message:
[refactor](expr) Replace Expr.toThrift() and normalize() with visitor pattern (#61165)

Replace toThrift(TExprNode) and normalize(Normalizer) methods in Expr
and all 39 subclasses with ExprToThriftVisitor and ExprNormalizeVisitor,
following the same visitor pattern used for toSql() in commit
ac3ff9173d.

Key changes:
- Create ExprToThriftVisitor with 39 visitXxx methods + static
treeToThrift/treesToThrift utilities
- Create ExprNormalizeVisitor in planner.normalize package with custom
SlotRef/FunctionCallExpr handling
- Remove toThrift(), treeToThrift(), treesToThrift(), normalize(),
opcode field, getOpcode() from Expr.java
- Remove TExprOpcode from
ArithmeticExpr/BinaryPredicate/CompoundPredicate/MatchPredicate Operator
enums
- Update ~60 call sites across planner, datasource, nereids, and qe
packages
- Move SearchPredicate thrift helpers (buildThriftParam,
buildDslAstExplainLines) to visitor
- Update external getOpcode() callers to use BinaryPredicate.Operator
directly
- Add ExprToThriftBehaviorTest (18 tests) covering representative Expr
types

After this change, Expr and its subclasses have zero dependencies on
org.apache.doris.planner.normalize.Normalizer and thrift types used
solely for serialization (TExprNode, TExprNodeType, TExprOpcode, etc).


Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: skip detail fetch for sold slugs in discover mode

In discover mode, sold slugs (~94K) previously triggered detail fetches
that all resulted in redirect_mismatch errors and took ~15 hours.

Changes:
- _collect_discovery_slugs() now returns (active_slugs, sold_slugs, ...)
  where active=for_sale+auction, sold separate (not fetched)
- New _push_status_update() bulk-marks sold slugs via API status-update
  endpoint without fetching their detail pages
- run_once() uses only active_slugs for detail phase, then calls
  _push_status_update for sold_slugs
- Tests updated for new 6-element tuple signature + new regression test

Expected discover time: hours -> ~15 min for active slug detail fetch

commit.message:
CI: use CMAKE_FIND_ROOT_PATH for Android Qt discovery

The Android NDK toolchain.cmake sets CMAKE_FIND_ROOT_PATH_MODE_PACKAGE=ONLY
which makes find_package() ignore CMAKE_PREFIX_PATH entirely. The correct
variable for Qt cross-compilation on Android is CMAKE_FIND_ROOT_PATH, as
shown in the Qt 6 Android documentation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
